channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end DEI practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump will issue executive orders to remake immigration policies; legal, logistical questions remain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is going to issue a series of orders aimed at remaking America’s immigration policies, ending asylum access, sending troops to the southern border and ending birthright citizenship, an incoming White House official said.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, SOPHIA TAREEN and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump inauguration to kick off 100-day rush
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to be sworn in at the Capitol on Monday, solidifying his stunning return to the White House and kicking off what is expected to be a busy 100-day rush focused on a handful of hot-button issues.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump vows to release JFK, RFK, MLK assassination records
item.description: Trump vowed to release records related to former President John F. Kennedy, Robert F. Kennedy and Martin Luther King Jr.’s assassinations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: South African police say ringleader in illegal gold mining disaster allowed to escape by officers
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — A suspected illegal gold mining ringleader escaped from custody with help from police officers about a week after resurfacing from a disused mine in South Africa where 246 survivors were rescued and 78 bodies were retrieved last week, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci and Milley in an effort to guard against potential 'revenge' by Trump
item.description: Dr. Anthony Fauci and retired Gen. Mark Milley say they appreciate their pardons from President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia man's loved ones sue Sealy Mattress company after he died inside locked trailer at facility: lawsuit
item.description: The loved ones of a Georgia man who died after being locked inside a trailer at a Sealy Mattress facility have filed a lawsuit against the company.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump to be sworn in as the 47th president and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Millions across the US brace for plummeting temperatures and winter storms
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Residents across the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine are bracing for dangerously low temperatures as tens of millions of residents along the East Coast contend with a thick blanket of snow — and more snowfall in the forecast.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: From backing a ban to being hailed as a savior: Inside Trump's TikTok shift
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — During his first term as president, Donald Trump led the effort to ban TikTok, the hugely popular video-sharing site he said posed a threat to U.S. national security. But on the eve of his return to the White House, the president-elect is being hailed as the app's savior.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok restores service for US users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: TikTok restored service to users in the United States on Sunday just hours after the popular video-sharing platform went dark in response to a federal ban, which President-elect Donald Trump said he would try to pause by executive order on his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Content creators respond to TikTok's uncertain future in the US
item.description: TikTok content creators who make a living promoting their businesses and personal brands on the app were afraid Sunday that they would have to find new ways to engage consumers and make money as the app's future in the United States remained uncertain. TikTok told U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Can you spot black ice? What to do if you start sliding or skidding
item.description: Black ice forms without creating bubbles, meaning it blends in with the road and becomes hard to detect.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, Fox 5

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Legacy news on its heels as second Trump era begins, but not abandoning mission
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As Donald Trump prepares to assume the presidency for a second time, he faces a news establishment on its heels — but not flat on its back. Last year's presidential campaign suggested that many traditional outlets are not depended upon, or trusted, nearly as much as they used to be.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok says it's in the process of restoring service to US users
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States were no longer able to watch or post videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular video-sharing app took effect Sunday amid questions over how long the blackout might last.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why are lone homes left standing after the fires? It's not entirely luck
item.description: Emails and videos of burned buildings in Los Angeles next to those left standing have been flying back and forth among architects, builders and fire safety specialists around the world.
item.dc:creator: ED DAVEY and INGRID LOBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump inauguration on alert for lone wolf attacks: how to spot a bad actor
item.description: An expert weighed in on what law enforcement keep an eye on to spot potential lone wolf attackers during the Inauguration festivities for President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington retires its first women's jersey, sending Kelsey Plum's No. 10 to the rafters
item.description: It was always a matter of when — not if — Washington would retire Kelsey Plum’s number. Eight years after she graduated, it happened.
item.dc:creator: JOSH KIRSHENBAUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ will give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: President-elect Trump told NBC News on Saturday that he will likely give TikTok a 90-day extension while his incoming administration assesses the situation following the Supreme Court decision to uphold a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Family of Utah student who died hiking in Hawaii over winter break speaks out
item.description: "I know her goals in life were to protect lands and promote a healthy place to live for all of us. I can just speak in volumes to what an amazing, beautiful, strong woman she is.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Boneza, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok says it will 'go dark' unless it gets clarity from Biden following Supreme Court ruling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — TikTok said it will have to “go dark” this weekend unless the outgoing Biden administration assures the company it won’t enforce a shutdown of the popular app after the Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld the federal law banning the app unless it’s sold by its China-based pa
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Extreme cold forecast for much of US in coming days
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Another blast of winter weather is expected in parts of the U.S. in the coming days, including bone-chilling wind in the Northern Plains and unusual snow and ice in the Gulf Coast area. Cold weather forecasted for Monday for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago prepares for deportation arrests targeting hundreds next week after Trump takes office
item.description: Federal immigration officers will target more than 300 people with histories of egregious, violent crimes after President-elect Donald Trump takes office Monday, an official said, marking his administration's initial attempt toward fulfilling his promise of large-scale deportations.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of missing pregnant woman reveals suspicions about disappearance
item.description: An Indiana woman, nine months pregnant at the time, disappeared over three months ago, and her family has suspicions about her whereabouts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judges allow some Capitol riot defendants to return to DC for Trump's inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of Donald Trump supporters stormed the U.S. Capitol after he lost the 2020 presidential election. Four years later, some of them are allowed to return to the nation's capital so they can celebrate Trump's return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Need to get a tattoo removed? What to know about the risks, the cost and more
item.description: When you’re young and carefree, a spur-of-the-moment tattoo feels like the ultimate form of self-expression. Years later, though, you might have a change of heart.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: One dead after early Saturday morning shooting in Ohio
item.description: A sergeant on the scene confirmed to NewsNation affiliate WDTN that the death is being investigated by the homicide unit.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, NewsNation

item.title: On LA fire lines, inmates shoulder heavy packs and tackle dangerous work for less than $30 a day
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Sal Almanza has worked 24-hour shifts in grueling terrain, cutting fire lines and hauling away brush trying to keep ahead of fires that have devastated several Los Angeles neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judges hand down what could be the last punishments for the Jan. 6 riot before pardons from Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge who oversaw Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case denounced efforts to rewrite the history of the U.S. Capitol attack Friday as she handed down what she acknowledged may be one of the final punishments for the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump inauguration moved indoors because of freezing temperatures
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration ceremony is being moved indoors because of expected bitter cold Monday in Washington, D.C. Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance are expected to be sworn in inside the Capitol Rotunda rather than on the West Front of the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Fire at one of the world's largest battery plants forces evacuations in California
item.description: Hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate and part of Highway 1 in Northern California was closed after a major fire erupted at one of the world's largest battery storage plants.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
